medical_history.chief_complaint: Episodes of right-sided facial twitching and increasing lethargy while at school, progressing to right hemiparesis over a 4-hour time course
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Previously diagnosed 2 years prior with a left temporal lesion on imaging obtained for hearing loss, but had never seen a neurosurgeon and further workup was not done at that time

physical_examination.general_examination: Facial asymmetry with a right facial droop and profound right hemiparesis

radiographic_examinations.date: 2 years prior to the acute haemorrhage event
radiographic_examinations.findings: Left frontal peri-insular developmental venous anomaly
Left frontotemporal hypoattenuation initially thought to be a haemorrhagic tumour, with abnormal tortuous vessels on CT angiography
Concerning for a large thrombosed cortical vein
Collection of vessels draining into the superior sagittal sinus via the vein of Trolard, concerning for a DVA

other_tests.findings: Used to wean the patient off propofol
